Hey, I'm looking at the audit from the Rotterdam warehouse for the Q3 electronic component stock. We've got some weird discrepancies between our WMS and the physical count. Can you look at this data and tell me why we might be seeing these small gaps?

Item ID | Expected | Physical | Variance
E-992-X | 450 | 448 | -2
E-104-B | 1200 | 1197 | -3
E-443-S | 85 | 84 | -1
E-772-K | 310 | 307 | -3

The counts happen every month but it's always just a few units.

pool.baseline:
hey i keep getting a 500 error on the /api/v1/checkout endpoint whenever a user tries to apply a discount code. here is the relevant snippet from the logs:

[2023-10-27 14:22:01] Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/app/services/checkout_service.py", line 142, in apply_discount
    discount_val = discount_engine.calculate(cart_total, code)
  File "/app/services/discount_engine.py", line 58, in calculate
    return self._apply_seasonal_logic(base_price, code)
  File "/app/services/discount_engine.py", line 89, in _apply_seasonal_logic
    raise KeyError(f"Invalid seasonal modifier for code: {code}")
KeyError: 'Invalid seasonal modifier for code: FALL20'

seems like it's just failing on that specific code. any idea why it's hitting a KeyError there instead of just returning a validation error?
